_design_intent: Clean EventContract on a CFTC DCM. Expected: parser OK; UPI NO_PRODUCT_DEFINITION; CFTC CONDITIONAL; EMIR NOT_APPLICABLE. Tests the 'novel-but-clean' path: a structurally well-formed event contract that still cannot be assigned a UPI because the taxonomy does not cover it.
description: Binary event contract paying USD 1.00 per contract if the EU formally approves a Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism threshold increase before 31 December 2027.
event_description: EU approval of CBAM threshold increase before 2027-12-31
underlying_economic_exposure: EU steel manufacturer whose import cost structure depends on CBAM threshold ruling

_design_intent: Worst-case EventContract: offshore venue, missing UTI, invalid timestamp, non-ISO settlement currency, missing other-counterparty LEI. Expected: parser PARTIAL on timestamp; UPI NO_PRODUCT_DEFINITION; Module 3 multiple non-compliant findings under both CFTC and EMIR. Demonstrates the full stack of regulatory invisibility.
description: Binary event contract paying USDC 1.00 if Marine Le Pen's Rassemblement National wins the 2027 French presidential election second round.
event_description: RN wins French presidential second round in 2027
underlying_economic_exposure: EU asset manager with French sovereign bond exposure

_design_intent: Conventional Rates swap with intentional UTI namespace mismatch: the first 20 characters of the UTI do not equal the reporting_counterparty_lei. Expected: parser OK; UPI MATCHED_WITH_WARNINGS or MATCHED; Module 3 reports UTI namespace_mismatch finding. Demonstrates the Module 3 UTI namespace validation step.

Conventional Credit Default Swap with all EMIR margin fields set to null. Expected: parser OK; UPI MATCHED or MATCHED_WITH_WARNINGS; Module 3 reports EMIR_MARGIN_NULL non-compliant finding (null is not the same as 0 under EMIR Refit / MAS). Demonstrates the EMIR margin trap from the lecture.

Fully clean FX Forward. Expected: parser OK; UPI MATCHED (or MATCHED_WITH_WARNINGS for one defaulted attribute); Module 3 fully compliant under both CFTC and EMIR. Acts as the 'green path' control case in the additional-trades set.
